2014-11-01.title: Transcript of New York Times Interview With President Ma Ying-jeou of Taiwan
2014-11-01.abstract: An official transcript from the office of President Ma Ying-jeou of Taiwan of an interview with The New York Times.

2014-11-01.title: On Visit, Obama to Face a Backsliding Myanmar
2014-11-01.abstract: President Obama has pressed the Burmese leader on the pace of constitutional reforms and on the treatment of religious minorities.

2014-11-01.title: Street-Level Views of the Protests in Burkina Faso
2014-11-01.abstract: Video posted on YouTube offered vivid glimpses of the demonstrators who forced Burkina Faso’s longtime president, Blaise Compaoré, from office.

2014-11-01.title: With Political Rift Still Deep, Russia-Ukraine Gas Deal Is Widely Seen as a Patch
2014-11-01.abstract: The agreement was reached after both sides accepted the financial realities, in the face of an approaching winter.

2014-11-01.title: Spy Allegations in a Presidential Race Conjure Romania’s Authoritarian Past
2014-11-01.abstract: A spying allegation tapped into a rich vein of Romanian political culture clogged with accusations and counter-accusations of undercover skulduggery.

2014-11-02.title: Labour Party Leader Backs Replacing House of Lords
2014-11-02.abstract: Ed Miliband, the Labour leader, proposed replacing the House of Lords, which traces its roots to the 14th century, with an American-style elected Senate.

2014-11-02.title: Netanyahu Urges Restraint in Row Over Sacred Site
2014-11-02.abstract: Several lawmakers from Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s Likud Party have been among a small but vocal group advocating increased Jewish access and prayer at the site, known to Jews as the Temple Mount.

2014-11-02.title: Argentine Judge Orders Arrest of Spanish Ex-Officials
2014-11-02.abstract: The judge invoked universal jurisdiction in the case of 20 Franco-era officials accused of torturing dissidents.

2014-11-02.title: China Approves Security Law Emphasizing Counterespionage
2014-11-02.abstract: The change, made official on Saturday, targets foreign spies and their domestic collaborators, reflecting top-level concerns about growing political threats from abroad, analysts said.

2014-11-02.title: U.N. Panel Issues Its Starkest Warning Yet on Global Warming
2014-11-02.abstract: Failure to reduce emissions could threaten society with food shortages, refugee crises, major flooding and mass extinctions, the group of scientists and other experts found.

2014-11-03.title: In Korean Baseball, Louder Cheers and More Squid
2014-11-03.abstract: Professional baseball is thriving in South Korea, with the festivities in the stands both raucous and organized to a degree that American fans might find startling.

2014-11-03.title: Seeing a New Future for French Nuclear Site, After the Toxic Dust Has Settled
2014-11-03.abstract: Scientists blew up more than half a ton of uranium in 2,000 explosions at a fort only 14 miles from the Eiffel Tower. Now there is disagreement over whether the site should be redeveloped.

2014-11-03.title: Woman in Iran Sent to Prison After Going to Sports Event
2014-11-03.abstract: An Iranian-British woman was arrested and later detained after attending a men’s volleyball match. Now she has received a one-year prison sentence.

2014-11-03.title: Keeping Mexico’s Revolutionary Fires Alive
2014-11-03.abstract: A small teacher training college has been at the center of a national crisis in Mexico since 43 of its students disappeared after a violent confrontation with the police.

2014-11-03.title: Egyptian Journalists Protest Editors’ Pledge Not to Criticize State
2014-11-03.abstract: Hundreds objected to a statement from top newspaper editors that they would refrain from publishing articles critical of the government during a time of fierce fighting with militant extremists.

2014-11-04.title: Burkina Faso: A Regional Warning
2014-11-04.abstract: The African Union on Monday gave Burkina Faso’s military leaders two weeks to return power to a civilian-led government or face severe sanctions.

2014-11-04.title: Egyptian Cleric Defends Forced Evacuation of Families From Sinai
2014-11-04.abstract: The government-appointed mufti, or chief interpreter of Islamic law, defended the clearing out of more than 1,100 families from the peninsula to root out extremists.

2014-11-04.title: Merkel Toughens Position on Britain’s E.U. Demands
2014-11-04.abstract: Prime Minister David Cameron of Britain, under mounting pressure from anti-European populists, continued to insist on new measures limiting the right of Europeans to live and work there.

2014-11-04.title: For a Packed Part of Jerusalem, Expansion Plans Have Built Mostly Outrage
2014-11-04.abstract: An Israeli committee’s approval of about 500 new apartments for settlers in Ramat Shlomo on Monday brought condemnation from the Obama administration.

2014-11-04.title: Role for Russia Gives Iran Talks a Possible Boost
2014-11-04.abstract: Iran has tentatively agreed to ship much of its huge stockpile of uranium to Russia if it reaches a broader nuclear deal with the West.

2014-11-05.title: Ukraine: President Orders Forces Bolstered in the Separatist East
2014-11-05.abstract: President Petro O. Poroshenko has ordered the military to send reinforcements to Ukraine’s east.

2014-11-05.title: Kashmir Protests Follow Shootings
2014-11-05.abstract: Protests broke out in Indian-administered Kashmir on Tuesday, a day after Indian soldiers fatally shot two people in a car that did not stop at a checkpoint.

2014-11-05.title: Amnesty International Says Israel Showed ‘Callous Indifference’ in Gaza
2014-11-05.abstract: Amnesty International published a report accusing Israel of war crimes in its 50-day war with Hamas in the Gaza Strip this summer.

2014-11-05.title: Pakistani Christian Couple Are Tortured and Burned to Death by Angry Mob
2014-11-05.abstract: The authorities planned to arrest 460 people in the killings, which began when the couple were accused of blasphemy by burning the Quran.

2014-11-05.title: Saudi Arabia Takes Action After Shiites Are Attacked
2014-11-05.abstract: Security officials moved on Tuesday to crush an outbreak of violence that included what the Interior Ministry called a terrorist ambush on mosque worshipers in a minority Shiite community.

2014-11-06.title: Chinese President’s  Delegation Tied to Illegal Ivory Purchases  During Africa Visit
2014-11-06.abstract: At a time when China says it is trying to root out corruption, a report accuses President Xi Jinping’s delegation of colluding with corrupt Tanzanian officials to smuggle ivory.

2014-11-06.title: In Love With a Priest: Support Groups Spread
2014-11-06.abstract: Challengers point to a shortage of Roman Catholic priests and to studies that show celibacy is a deterrent for young men wanting to enter the priesthood.

2014-11-06.title: Switzerland: Next Director Is Named for CERN Nuclear Laboratory
2014-11-06.abstract: Fabiola Gianotti, who led one of the two teams of physicists that discovered the Higgs boson, the so-called God particle that imbues other particles with mass, has been named the next director general of CERN, the European Organization for Nuclear Research.

2014-11-06.title: The Hague: Serbian Nationalist Leader With Cancer May Be Freed Temporarily
2014-11-06.abstract: The United Nations war crimes tribunal in The Hague has proposed a temporary release for Vojislav Seselj, a Serbian nationalist leader with cancer, whose health has seriously deteriorated during his long-running trial.

2014-11-06.title: Obama to Seek Congressional Backing for Military Campaign Against ISIS
2014-11-06.abstract: The president opened the door to a lengthy, potentially contentious debate over the nature and extent of American engagement in Iraq and Syria.

2014-11-07.title: Q. and A.: Andrew Wedeman on the Fight Against Corruption in China
2014-11-07.abstract: The professor of political science at Georgia State University explains the background and implications of the anticorruption drive led by the Communist Party leader Xi Jinping.

2014-11-07.title: Iran: Detained Reporter Is Charged, but May Be Freed Soon, Official Says
2014-11-07.abstract: The Iranian authorities have charged a detained Washington Post reporter, Jason Rezaian, with being “involved in activities beyond journalism,” a senior Iranian emissary said in a television interview on Thursday.

2014-11-07.title: Russia: Putin Defends Soviet-Nazi Pact
2014-11-07.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin has revised his opinion of an important piece of Soviet history, calling the 1939 Molotov-Ribbentrop pact that Moscow signed with Nazi Germany to divide up Eastern Europe not so bad.

2014-11-07.title: Libyan Supreme Court Rules Parliament Invalid
2014-11-07.abstract: Libya’s Supreme Court ruled that the country’s Parliament had been seated unconstitutionally. The governing coalition said the court had been influenced by a rival faction.

2014-11-07.title: Hague Prosecutor Cites Possible Israeli War Crimes in Gaza Flotilla Raid
2014-11-07.abstract: The court says that Israeli forces may have committed war crimes in 2010 when they stormed a civilian flotilla delivering aid but that the consequences did not warrant a criminal investigation.

2014-11-08.title: U.S. Embassy in Bahamas Issues Crime Warning
2014-11-08.abstract: Water scooter operators are reported to have sexually assaulted at least three Americans since July, the embassy said in a security alert, the latest in a series of advisories about crime.

2014-11-08.title: To China, Shift in Obama’s Political Fortunes Eclipses U.S. Economic Gains
2014-11-08.abstract: When President Obama visits China on Monday, authorities there are likely to dwell more on his electoral reversals than on the robust American economy, analysts say.

2014-11-08.title: Drug Gang Killed Students, Mexican Law Official Says
2014-11-08.abstract: Members of a drug gang arrested in connection with the disappearance of 43 college students told investigators that they had killed the students and burned their bodies, the Mexican attorney general said.

2014-11-08.title: Obama Urged Iranian Leader to See Common Interests
2014-11-08.abstract: The president, in an October letter to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, said that a nuclear agreement and supporting the fight against the Islamic State would help Iran.

2014-11-08.title: Latvian Minister Declares He’s Gay, Exposing Fault Lines of New Culture War in Europe
2014-11-08.abstract: Differing reactions to Foreign Minister Edgars Rinkevics’s declaration seemed to reveal the contours of a cultural fault line between East and West.

2014-11-09.title: Powerful Afghan Police Chief Puts Fear in Taliban and Their Enemies
2014-11-09.abstract: Lt. Gen. Abdul Raziq has effectively checked the insurgents while being accused of human rights abuses, presenting Afghanistan’s new president with a dilemma.

2014-11-09.title: Getting Close to Terror, but Not to Stop It
2014-11-09.abstract: Despite the C.I.A.'s concerns, a Port Authority officer working with the F.B.I. kept ties to sources inside the Sunni terrorist group Jundallah.

2014-11-09.title: Explosions Rock Afghan Capital After Months of Calm
2014-11-09.abstract: In one attack, an explosive detonated near a bus carrying soldiers; in another, a suicide bomber infiltrated Police Headquarters. Casualties appeared to be limited.

2014-11-09.title: On a Memory-Filled Date, the Fall of the Berlin Wall Stands Front and Center
2014-11-09.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel led Germany in celebrations flavored with a mix of triumph and tragedy to commemorate the fall of the wall on Nov. 9, 1989.

2014-11-09.title: 1889: London Celebrates Lord Mayor’s Day
2014-11-09.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: Sir Henry Isaacs celebrates his election as Lord Mayor with a parade in London in 1889.

2014-11-10.title: China’s ‘New Type’ of Ties Fails to Sway Obama
2014-11-10.abstract: President Obama has said he welcomes China’s rise, but he has been less keen about the “new type of great power relations” that Xi Jinping says he wants.

2014-11-10.title: U.S. Gives North Korea the Silent Treatment
2014-11-10.abstract: By sending the nation’s top spy to win the release of two American prisoners, President Obama signaled that he would not reward the North for their freedom.

2014-11-10.title: Obama Arrives in China on Trip With Complex Agenda
2014-11-10.abstract: The president plunged into a hectic schedule that mixed the solemn rituals of a state visit with the deal-making of an economic summit meeting.

2014-11-10.title: In Ukraine, Shelling and Convoys of Armed Trucks Threaten Cease-Fire
2014-11-10.abstract: European monitors confirmed reports of unmarked military vehicles driving through rebel-held territory over the weekend while Donetsk, the region’s biggest city, endured an artillery battle.

2014-11-10.title: Judgment Awaits for Crew of South Korea Ferry
2014-11-10.abstract: On Tuesday the court is scheduled to announce verdicts and sentences, which could include the death penalty.

2014-11-11.title: Sierra Leone Defends Jailing Critic of Ebola Response
2014-11-11.abstract: The attorney general in Sierra Leone said a prominent radio journalist had undermined the government’s effort to stop the disease’s spread.

2014-11-11.title: Work-for-Welfare Gains a Foothold in Hungary
2014-11-11.abstract: Under the right-wing rule of Prime Minister Viktor Orban, the unemployed in Halmaj — a poor, rural village of 1,800 — must work to get more public assistance.

2014-11-11.title: Russian News Agency Expands Global Reach
2014-11-11.abstract: The major expansion of Russia’s state news agency into the West, including capitals like Washington and Berlin, is intended to counter what Russia describes as Western propaganda.

2014-11-11.title: For Guccifer, Hacking Was Easy. Prison Is Hard.
2014-11-11.abstract: Marcel-Lehel Lazar, whose pseudonym celebrated “the style of Gucci and the light of Lucifer,” rampaged through the email of rich Americans, showing the ease of going rogue online.

2014-11-11.title: Malala Yousafzai, Nobel Laureate, Is Assailed by Schools Group in Pakistan
2014-11-11.abstract: A network of private schools on Monday called for a day of protest against Ms. Yousafzai, 17, and her memoir about being shot by a Taliban gunman after pressing for girls’ rights and education.

2014-11-12.title: U.N. Seeks a More Nimble Response to Ebola in Africa
2014-11-12.abstract: The organization has struggled to keep up with the spread and decline of the disease in specific localities.

2014-11-12.title: Mexico: Protesters Burn Party Office
2014-11-12.abstract: Teachers and students protesting the abduction and apparent murder of 43 college students in September set fire Tuesday to the headquarters of President Enrique Peña Nieto’s party in Guerrero State.

2014-11-12.title: Guyana: Legislature Is Suspended
2014-11-12.abstract: President Donald Ramotar of Guyana used a constitutional mechanism that gives him the right to disband the legislature for up to six months.

2014-11-12.title: Mali: New Ebola Case Is Confirmed as Response to First Was Wrapping Up
2014-11-12.abstract: Mali, which was just coming to the end of 21-day quarantines for 108 people linked to its first Ebola case, now has a second, the government announced Tuesday.

2014-11-12.title: Politics Mingles With Art at a Palestinian Cultural Festival
2014-11-12.abstract: The festival, Qalandiya International, faced special challenges in catering to a fragmented Palestinian society struggling against Israel and recovering from the 50-day Gaza war.

2014-11-13.title: Sedition Fight in Malaysia
2014-11-13.abstract: A British colonial-era law is being used to charge and investigate politicians, activists, academics and students in Malaysia. But a law professor is challenging its constitutionality.

2014-11-13.title: Life Imitates Propaganda in Egypt
2014-11-13.abstract: Talking politics with a foreigner in a cafe in Cairo now strikes some Egyptians as grounds for arrest.

2014-11-13.title: Pakistan Seeks Dissidents’ Arrest
2014-11-13.abstract: Warrants were issued for the opposition leaders Imran Khan and Muhammad Tahir-ul Qadri for their part in leading a protest movement.

2014-11-13.title: Germany: 9 Held in Antiterrorism Raids
2014-11-13.abstract: The police in the Cologne area detained nine men early Wednesday and searched at least 20 homes in raids against people suspected of supporting terrorism.

2014-11-13.title: Iran: Pentagon Rebuts Claim on Drone
2014-11-13.abstract: Iran’s claim to have not only replicated but test-flown its version of a sophisticated bat-winged American spy drone captured in late 2011 is a gross exaggeration, the Pentagon said Wednesday.

2014-11-14.title: For a Liberian Family, Ebola Turns Loving Care Into Deadly Risk
2014-11-14.abstract: The Doryen family, unbowed survivors of Liberia’s civil war, saw seven members die from the virus in a matter of months and lost its sense of unity.

2014-11-14.title: Psychologists to Review Role in Detainee Interrogations
2014-11-14.abstract: The nation’s largest organization of psychologists will investigate whether it supported the government’s use of torture in the interrogation of prisoners during the Bush administration.

2014-11-14.title: Portugal: Legionnaires’ Disease Alert
2014-11-14.abstract: An outbreak of Legionnaires’ disease has killed at least five people and sickened more than 300 in Portugal, the World Health Organization reported Thursday.

2014-11-14.title: Turkey: After an Attack on U.S. Sailors, 12 Suspects Are Charged and Released
2014-11-14.abstract: Twelve Turks arrested in Istanbul in connection with a videotaped assault on three visiting American sailors were charged on Thursday with insult, inflicting injury and violating Turkey’s protest and rally law, but all of them were released, Turkey’s Anadolu news agency reported.

2014-11-14.title: Kerry Is Optimistic After Meeting Over Holy Site in Jerusalem
2014-11-14.abstract: John Kerry met with the leaders of Jordan, Israel and the Palestinian Authority to discuss the site known to Muslims as the Noble Sanctuary and to Jews as Temple Mount.

2014-11-15.title: Islamic State Says It Plans to Issue Its Own Currency
2014-11-15.abstract: The leaders of the Islamic State have created their own currency, part of a grandiose plan to restore the caliphate era that dominated the Middle East more than 1,300 years ago.

2014-11-15.title: Algeria: President Hospitalized in France, Reports Say
2014-11-15.abstract: President Abdelaziz Bouteflika, who was hospitalized for months last year after a stroke, has been admitted to a French hospital in the city of Grenoble, news agencies reported Friday, citing French police officials.

2014-11-15.title: Rare Vaccine-Derived Polio Discovered in 2 Countries
2014-11-15.abstract: Cases of paralysis caused by mutating polio vaccine have been found in South Sudan and Madagascar, the World Health Organization said Friday.

2014-11-15.title: ISIS ‘Slave Market Day’
2014-11-15.abstract: In a video posted in October 2014 on YouTube, a group of men believed to be Islamic State fighters are shown sitting in a room bantering about buying and selling Yazidi girls on “slave market day.”

2014-11-15.title: Yazidi Girls Seized by ISIS Speak Out After Escape
2014-11-15.abstract: A few of the thousands of young women from the minority Yazidi religion who were kidnapped by the Islamic State told of a systemized industry of slavery.

2014-11-16.title: Iraq and U.S. Find Some Potential Sunni Allies Have Already Been Lost
2014-11-16.abstract: The United States and the Iraqi government want to enlist Sunni tribes to fight the Islamic State, but many of them have already been co-opted or conquered.

2014-11-16.title: Obama Says Russia’s Arming of Separatists Breaks Pact With Ukraine
2014-11-16.abstract: President Obama edged closer to describing Russia’s military incursions in Ukraine as an invasion and said Russians were supplying heavy arms to separatists in violation of an agreement Moscow signed.

2014-11-16.title: Kabul Attack Intended for Lawmaker Kills 3
2014-11-16.abstract: The lawmaker who was apparently the target of the attack, Shukria Barakzai, is an outspoken legislator who has campaigned for women’s rights and has run underground schools for girls.

2014-11-16.title: Obama Calls Islamic State’s Killing of Peter Kassig ‘Pure Evil’
2014-11-16.abstract: President Obama called the execution of Mr. Kassig, a 26-year-old aid worker, “an act of pure evil” after it was revealed in a video on Sunday.

2014-11-16.title: 1889: Upheaval in Brazil
2014-11-16.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: A revolution broke out in Brazil in 1889.

2014-11-17.title: Public Grieving for Pop Singer Is Startling for Iran
2014-11-17.abstract: Iranian authorities were caught off guard over the weekend when thousands of people joined spontaneous gatherings around the country to mourn and remember a pop singer who died of cancer at age 30.

2014-11-17.title: Palestinian Shot by Israeli Troops at Gaza Border
2014-11-17.abstract: A spokeswoman for the Israeli hospital where the wounded Palestinian was taken for treatment said he was a 10-year-old boy.

2014-11-17.title: In Public Land Sale, Not All of Spain Is Buying
2014-11-17.abstract: There has been a rise in proposed land deals that politicians hope will fill treasuries and that critics see as a possible repeat of a real estate bubble that led to an economic crisis.

2014-11-17.title: Favorite Concedes Presidency in Romania
2014-11-17.abstract: Prime Minister Victor Ponta conceded Romania’s presidential runoff election late Sunday night to the center-right candidate, Klaus Iohannis, the mayor of the Transylvanian city of Sibiu.

2014-11-17.title: In New Napoleonic Era, His Hats and Stockings Rise to Power
2014-11-17.abstract: The prices of the emperor’s belongings have risen relentlessly, including a hat that sold for about $2.4 million at a Paris auction, making it difficult for French museums to compete.

2014-11-18.title: Egypt Will Expand Its Security Zone Near Gaza Strip
2014-11-18.abstract: The discovery of smuggling tunnels will lead to the doubling of a secured buffer zone in the border town of Rafah, officials said.

2014-11-18.title: Global Number of Youths Is Highest Ever, U.N. Reports
2014-11-18.abstract: A United Nations report said the fate of the world’s adolescent population depended on governments’ ability to provide health care, education and jobs.

2014-11-18.title: Sudan: Peace Talks Adjourn
2014-11-18.abstract: Talks between the Sudanese government and a rebel group adjourned in Ethiopia on Monday, to be reconvened at a date yet to be determined.

2014-11-18.title: Coal Rush in India Could Tip Balance on Climate Change
2014-11-18.abstract: The country has a voracious and growing demand for energy, and officials see coal as the best way forward. However, experts predict dire consequences.

2014-11-18.title: President of Colombia Halts Peace Talks With Rebels After General Is Seized
2014-11-18.abstract: Describing what occurred as a kidnapping, President Juan Manuel Santos said that he had directed the country’s peace negotiators not to travel to Havana for a new round of talks with the guerrillas.

2014-11-19.title: In a City Wary of Skyscrapers, a New Tower May Rise
2014-11-19.abstract: Plans for a 42-story landmark in a somewhat undistinguished part of Paris have raised the alarm for traditionalists who have not approved a new skyscraper in four decades.

2014-11-19.title: Norwegian Filmmakers Apologize for Fake Syria Video
2014-11-19.abstract: A short film, shot in the style of witness video from Syria and posted on YouTube, provoked a torrent of criticism.

2014-11-19.title: Jewish Victims, All From One Jerusalem Street, Were a Congregation’s ‘Wise Scholars’
2014-11-19.abstract: Four Jewish men killed Tuesday while praying at their neighborhood synagogue all lived on the same block, and three were rabbis — one the scion of a celebrated Hasidic dynasty.

2014-11-19.title: Anti-E.U. Party in Britain Strives for Wider Clout
2014-11-19.abstract: Even as it stands a good chance to win a second seat in Parliament, the U.K. Independence Party has lost the support of its founder, who deplores it as xenophobic.

2014-11-19.title: In Jerusalem’s ‘War of Neighbors,’ the Differences Are Not Negotiable
2014-11-19.abstract: Many worry that the cycle of violence may be worsened by the growing focus on a holy site that Jews and Muslims both revere.

2014-11-20.title: Meeting U.S. Envoy, Putin Appears to Soften His Tone
2014-11-20.abstract: Upon receiving the new American ambassador to Russia, President Vladimir V. Putin said Moscow and Washington could find avenues of cooperation despite recent tensions.

2014-11-20.title: Colombia: Agreement Is Reached to Release General Held by Rebels
2014-11-20.abstract: The country’s largest rebel group has agreed to free a captured army general and four other captives, officials said Wednesday.

2014-11-20.title: Protesters Return to Cairo’s Streets to Mark an Anniversary
2014-11-20.abstract: At least 48 protesters were arrested near Tahrir Square at rallies in memory of activists killed in clashes with the police in 2011.

2014-11-20.title: More Than a Battle, Kobani Is a Publicity War
2014-11-20.abstract: The fractious Kurdish diaspora has pulled together against ISIS in the hope of creating a homeland in the area that is technically part of Syria.

2014-11-20.title: Pakistani, Held in Iraq, Wins a Round in Court
2014-11-20.abstract: The plaintiff, who was detained in Iraq in 2004, says he was tortured on and off for a decade, first by British and then by American troops.

2014-11-21.title: Israelis Confiscate Weapons Bound for East Jerusalem
2014-11-21.abstract: The police arrested five Arab citizens of Israel after discovering two shipping containers with knives, swords and fireworks.

2014-11-21.title: The Duchess of Alba, an Unconventional Aristocrat, Dies at 88
2014-11-21.abstract: The flamboyant Spanish aristocrat was head of the five-century-old House of Alba. Her outspokenness and unconventional choices of marriage partners made her a media sensation.

2014-11-21.title: Ebola Spread Has Slowed in Liberia, C.D.C. Says
2014-11-21.abstract: The international response to the epidemic, coupled with more effective action by local communities, has stopped its exponential spread in Liberia, the agency said.

2014-11-21.title: 2 Worlds of Hong Kong, and a Fatal Intersection
2014-11-21.abstract: The recent deaths of two women cast a spotlight on how impoverished housemaids are drawn to the city’s seedy bars to earn money through prostitution.

2014-11-21.title: Militants in Benghazi Attack Tied to a Qaeda Affiliate
2014-11-21.abstract: A United Nations document in support of sanctions against a group accused in the attack on the U.S. diplomatic mission cites its support for Al Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb.

2014-11-22.title: In Zimbabwe’s Succession Battle, Mugabe Pulls the Strings
2014-11-22.abstract: President Robert Mugabe is presiding over — and possibly choreographing — an increasingly fierce internal scramble for the rewards of high office.

2014-11-22.title: Madagascar: Plague Outbreak Kills 40
2014-11-22.abstract: The World Health Organization reported that an outbreak of plague, the infamous scourge that killed millions in the Dark Ages, has been recorded in parts of Madagascar.

2014-11-22.title: In a Shift, Obama Extends U.S. Role in Afghan Combat
2014-11-22.abstract: President Obama’s order would allow American forces to carry out missions against the Taliban and other militant groups threatening American troops or the Afghan government, sources said.

2014-11-22.title: ISIS Releases New Propaganda Video
2014-11-22.abstract: In the latest video released by the Islamic State, the British hostage John Cantlie described a failed rescue attempt and encouraged the United States and Britain to negotiate with his captors.

2014-11-22.title: Britain: 12 Are Injured in Blast at Hotel
2014-11-22.abstract: More than a dozen were hurt in what the police said they suspected was a natural-gas explosion in the basement of a central London hotel just before midnight Friday.

2014-11-23.title: Prosecution of Uighur Students Underscores Perils of Chinese Clampdown
2014-11-23.abstract: At least three students are expected to stand trial on Tuesday on charges of having worked for the imprisoned activist Ilham Tohti.

2014-11-23.title: China Said to Turn Reef Into Airstrip in Disputed Water
2014-11-23.abstract: A report from IHS Jane’s says the operation is intended to coerce other countries to relinquish territorial claims in the South China Sea.

2014-11-23.title: Iran Nuclear Negotiators, Facing Key Differences, Weigh Extending Deadline
2014-11-23.abstract: Western and Iranian officials said on Sunday that with issues still unresolved on an agreement to curb Iran’s nuclear program, they might consider prolonging the talks beyond Monday.

2014-11-23.title: Blast at Game Kills Dozens in Afghanistan
2014-11-23.abstract: Explosives were detonated in a crowd gathered to watch a game between a village team and players from a neighboring district in Paktika Province.

2014-11-23.title: Survivors Pulled From Ruins After Quake in Japan
2014-11-23.abstract: A magnitude-6.7 quake near the city of Nagano injured at least 40 people, damaged dozens of buildings and forced hundreds into emergency shelters.

2014-11-24.title: Tunisia Vote for Leader Likely to Go to a Runoff
2014-11-24.abstract: Beji Caid Essebsi, a former prime minister, and Moncef Marzouki, the interim president, were leading but neither was expected to win a majority.

2014-11-24.title: Graft Hobbles Iraq’s Military in Fighting ISIS
2014-11-24.abstract: The Iraqi military and police forces had been so pillaged by their own leadership that they all but collapsed this spring in the face of the advancing Islamic State fighters.

2014-11-24.title: Iran Grants Bail to Woman From Britain Held 5 Months
2014-11-24.abstract: Ghoncheh Ghavami, 25, who has dual citizenship, is appealing a one-year sentence for a conviction that has been linked to activism.

2014-11-24.title: Freed & Fighting for China’s Environment
2014-11-24.abstract: The environmental activist Wu Lihong was jailed for speaking out about pollution in China’s Lake Tai. Freed in 2010, he is still speaking out about China’s challenges to protect its waterways.

2014-11-24.title: Despite Persecution, Guardian of Lake Tai Spotlights China’s Polluters
2014-11-24.abstract: Despite pressure from Beijing, factories continue to dump toxic waste into waterways because the cost of violating the rules is lower than the cost of compliance.

2014-11-25.title: Bombs Hit Sole Civilian Airport in Libyan Capital
2014-11-25.abstract: Commanders from Operation Dignity said they ordered the strikes because the airport and the capital had fallen under the control of the rival coalition Libya Dawn.

2014-11-25.title: At Spanish Enclave, a Debate Over What Makes a Border
2014-11-25.abstract: Spanish authorities, struggling to secure the borders to their enclaves in Africa, are facing a new legal challenge over the position of border fences.

2014-11-25.title: Report Faults Care of Peace Corps Volunteer
2014-11-25.abstract: The Peace Corps inspector general cited “cascading delays and failures in treatment” as a factor in the death in 2013 of Nick Castle, a volunteer who fell gravely ill in China.

2014-11-25.title: Video Analysis of Fatal West Bank Shooting Said to Implicate Israeli Officer
2014-11-25.abstract: Analysis of video of the May shooting suggested that the officer was to blame, the boy’s father and an Israeli who reconstructed the shooting said.

2014-11-25.title: As Bombing Toll Rises, Afghan Villagers Direct Anger at Government
2014-11-25.abstract: Villagers of Yahya Khel, where a bomber killed 61 people Sunday, expressed anger at the Taliban but also at a national government they felt had offered them little.

2014-11-26.title: Shakespeare Folio Discovered in France
2014-11-26.abstract: A first folio of Shakespeare’s plays — one of only 233 known examples — was discovered by chance in a public library near Calais, France.

2014-11-26.title: Inquiry Into Rape Claims Against Irish Republican Army
2014-11-26.abstract: Among those supplying information to the authorities is Gerry Adams, the president of Sinn Fein and a member of the Irish Parliament.

2014-11-26.title: Colombia: Rebels Free Two Soldiers
2014-11-26.abstract: The country’s main rebel group on Tuesday released two low-ranking soldiers captured after a recent clash with government troops, but it remained unclear when it would free a general and two others it seized separately.

2014-11-26.title: A Call to Save a 12th-Century Minaret, Heard Far and Wide
2014-11-26.abstract: The Minaret of Jam, in a mountainous, remote region of Afghanistan, is leaning more and more, and officials fear it could fall.

2014-11-26.title: U.S.-Led Raid Frees 8 Qaeda Hostages From a Yemeni Cave
2014-11-26.abstract: The freed captives were six Yemeni citizens, a Saudi and an Ethiopian, who were unharmed, Yemeni officials said in a statement.

2014-11-27.title: Sierra Leone to Eclipse Liberia in Ebola Cases
2014-11-27.abstract: The World Health Organization reported more than 600 new cases of Ebola in the week that ended Sunday, more than half of them in Sierra Leone.

2014-11-27.title: Egypt: Rafah Border Crossing Is Opened Temporarily
2014-11-27.abstract: Egypt temporarily opened its crossing on its border with Gaza on Wednesday for the first time in 30 days, allowing more than 250 Palestinians who had been stuck in Egypt to return to the coastal territory.

2014-11-27.title: Rebels in Yemen Strike at Rivals
2014-11-27.abstract: The Houthis seized control of a compound belonging to one of the country’s most powerful tribal clans, the Al-Ahmar tribe.

2014-11-27.title: Oligarchs of Eastern Europe Scoop Up Stakes in Media Companies
2014-11-27.abstract: Regional business groups, many strongly tied to their countries’ governments, are buying influential news outlets, raising alarms about press freedom.

2014-11-27.title: Germany: Merkel Repeats Tough Stance on Ukraine
2014-11-27.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel of Germany told Germany’s Parliament on Wednesday that no military solution to the crisis with Russia was possible, but also that there was no way to lift sanctions under the present circumstances.

2014-11-28.title: Conflicting Policies on Syria and Islamic State Erode U.S. Standing in Mideast
2014-11-28.abstract: There is a broad sense on all sides that President Obama’s policies on Syria and the Islamic State remain contradictory.

2014-11-28.title: Israel Says Hamas Ring Plotted West Bank Attacks
2014-11-28.abstract: The Shin Bet security agency says it broke up a network of operatives that sought to provoke a heavy-handed response from Israel in the West Bank that would undermine the Palestinian Authority there.

2014-11-28.title: As Mexican Border Town Tries to Move On, Some Are Stuck in Limbo
2014-11-28.abstract: Tijuana is promoting itself as a city on the rise, shaking off years of drug violence and declining tourism. But it remains a way station for the downtrodden heading for America or kicked out of it.

2014-11-28.title: A Tilt Toward NATO in Ukraine as Parliament Meets
2014-11-28.abstract: At the convening of a new Parliament in Ukraine, President Petro O. Poroshenko urged the repeal of the country’s nonaligned status and a move toward joining NATO.

2014-11-28.title: Mexican Leader, Facing Protests, Promises to Overhaul Policing
2014-11-28.abstract: President Enrique Peña Nieto’s plan makes forces answerable to state and federal authorities, though observers questioned the credibility of a president who has had scandals of his own.

2014-11-29.title: In Northern Ireland, a Wave of Immigrants Is Met With Fists
2014-11-29.abstract: Racially motivated attacks have increased at a time when open borders in the European Union have led waves of illegal and legal migrants to Britain and Ireland.

2014-11-29.title: In a Twitter Post, Malaysia Airlines Sends the Wrong Message
2014-11-29.abstract: A tweet that was meant to appeal to adventurous travelers served as an unintentional reminder that the airline has lost two planes this year.

2014-11-29.title: Palestinian Refuge for 6 Decades, Now Flooded From Syria
2014-11-29.abstract: The Syrian civil war has driven a wave of Syrians into Lebanon, roughly doubling the population of the Palestinian refugee camp of Shatila.

2014-11-29.title: Roberto Gómez Bolaños, Mexico’s Comedic Artist ‘Chespirito,’ Dies at 85
2014-11-29.abstract: The actor, writer and director was known around the Spanish-speaking world for his iconic characters and widely viewed television shows.

2014-11-29.title: Conservationists Continue Push to Block Hong Kong Ivory Sales
2014-11-29.abstract: Protesters gathered Saturday outside a Hong Kong department store that had been prominently selling ivory products, part of an effort to publicize the city’s key role in the trade.

2014-11-30.title: Qatar Frees U.S. Couple in Death of Daughter
2014-11-30.abstract: After being cleared in Qatar of wrongdoing in the death of their daughter, an American couple was held by immigration officials when trying to depart Doha’s airport.

2014-11-30.title: Kin of Thai Princess Stripped of Royal Name
2014-11-30.abstract: Police officers with links to the family are accused of corruption, and in an unusual twist, have been charged with lèse-majesté, because, the authorities said, they used symbols of the monarchy for their own benefit.

2014-11-30.title: 1889: Metropolitan Opera House Reopens in New York
2014-11-30.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: The Metropolitan Opera House reopens in New York in 1889.

2014-11-30.title: Police Break Siege of Government Offices in Hong Kong
2014-11-30.abstract: The police used pepper spray to try to subdue thousands of pro-democracy demonstrators who surged around the Hong Kong leader’s offices in an escalation of the protests.

2014-11-30.title: Netanyahu Hints at Possibility of New Elections
2014-11-30.abstract: Amid growing acrimony among the fractious partners of Israel’s governing coalition, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu also berated ministers for attacking him.
